artifacts.title: Ask ArcGIS Pro AI to Find the Right Tool for You
artifacts.desc: Esri's ArcGIS Pro now embeds an AI assistant that discovers and runs spatial analysis tools on your behalf, automating GIS workflows. This is a direct signal for Daniel's ArcGIS Pro usage and a strong editorial angle for the Tool Critic and Industry Lens pillars.
artifacts.relevance_why: Direct: ArcGIS Pro is in Daniel's stack; this AI-native feature changes how spatial workflows are initiated and is editorially relevant to Globe & Atlas readers.

artifacts.title: Build Your Own MCP Server: A Repo-Agnostic File Search Tool for AI Assistants
artifacts.desc: Step-by-step tutorial for building a Python-based MCP server that gives Claude and other AI assistants repo-agnostic file search capability. Directly applicable to Daniel's Claude Code CLI + Python stack and his 3-layer agent architecture.
artifacts.relevance_why: Direct: uses Python and Claude API; MCP servers extend Claude Code CLI, which is in Daniel's direct stack.

artifacts.title: everything-claude-code — Agent Harness Performance Optimization System
artifacts.desc: A comprehensive harness for Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor adding skills, persistent memory, security layers, and research-first development patterns — directly supercharging Daniel's Claude Code CLI workflow with structured agent improvements.
artifacts.relevance_why: Direct: Claude Code CLI is in Daniel's direct stack; this repo systematically extends it with memory and skill management.

artifacts.title: claude-mem — Claude Code Plugin for Persistent Session Memory
artifacts.desc: Automatically captures Claude Code session context, compresses it with Claude's agent SDK, and injects relevant memory into future sessions — solving the context-loss problem Daniel faces when working on long FME/Python pipelines across sessions.
artifacts.relevance_why: Direct: Claude Code CLI is in Daniel's stack; this plugin adds persistent memory that persists across the coding sessions he uses daily.

artifacts.title: Optimizing Temporal Resolution for Earth Observation and Geospatial AI
artifacts.desc: Explains how revisit frequency decisions in satellite constellation selection impact geospatial AI model quality — directly relevant to Daniel's Sentinel Hub API workflows and his editorial lens on satellite imagery decision-making.
artifacts.relevance_why: Direct: Sentinel Hub API and Sentinel-2/SAR imagery are in Daniel's stack; temporal resolution strategy governs how he queries and processes imagery.

artifacts.title: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Harness + ShapeV2 — Runtime Control for Agents
artifacts.desc: AWS public-preview launch of AgentCore — a managed agent runtime paired with ShapeV2 for policy-based tool permissions. Adjacent to Daniel's 3-layer agent architecture as a potential orchestration-layer alternative to his current Claude API + GitHub Actions setup.
artifacts.relevance_why: Adjacent: replaces or competes with the orchestration layer Daniel builds manually with Claude API; ShapeV2 policy model is directly comparable to his directives layer.

artifacts.title: Managing 150+ AI Agent Skills at Scale — What Broke, What I Built
artifacts.desc: Practitioner post on managing large autonomous agent skill libraries with Python and SQLite — including failure patterns at scale. Directly relevant to Daniel's agent orchestration work and provides concrete lessons for the Career Navigator pillar.
artifacts.relevance_why: Direct: Python and agent orchestration are in Daniel's stack; the SQLite-based skill registry pattern is applicable to his multi-agent builds.

artifacts.title: n8n — Fair-Code Workflow Automation with Native AI and 400+ Integrations
artifacts.desc: Self-hostable TypeScript workflow automation platform with visual builder and native AI nodes — a strong adjacent alternative to Daniel's GitHub Actions + Claude API pipeline, especially for orchestrating GIS data ingestion tasks with visual debugging.
artifacts.relevance_why: Adjacent: directly replaces GitHub Actions for workflow orchestration with a visual AI-native layer; TypeScript aligns with Daniel's frontend stack.

artifacts.title: google-gemini/gemini-cli — Open-Source AI Agent for the Terminal
artifacts.desc: Google's open-source terminal agent powered by Gemini, built in TypeScript — a direct competitor to Claude Code CLI that Daniel already uses daily and worth benchmarking for GIS scripting tasks.
artifacts.relevance_why: Adjacent: directly competes with Claude Code CLI in Daniel's stack; monitoring this is required for accurate Tool Critic coverage of the terminal AI agent space.

artifacts.title: TraceMind v3 — AI Agent That Diagnoses LLM Quality Drops
artifacts.desc: Python-based LLMOps agent that detects hallucination regressions, runs A/B tests, and traces root causes of quality drops in LLM pipelines. Relevant to Daniel's Claude API workflows where output quality monitoring is increasingly important at scale.
artifacts.relevance_why: Adjacent: extends Daniel's Claude API usage with observability tooling he currently lacks; Python-native and applicable to his existing pipeline architecture.

artifacts.title: Welcome to GIS4Everyone — Remote Sensing, AI & Geospatial Science Channel
artifacts.desc: Launch video for a new YouTube channel dedicated to AI-powered remote sensing and GIS analysis — an emerging content creator in Daniel's direct editorial space worth tracking as a peer signal.
artifacts.relevance_why: Direct: GIS/remote sensing AI content is Daniel's editorial territory; this channel is a potential competitor and collaboration signal for Globe & Atlas.

artifacts.title: MicasenseMX2 Camera Returns Images with Striping — Stack Exchange GIS
artifacts.desc: Practitioner question about horizontal data artifacts in a 10-band multispectral drone camera — a real-world sensor calibration and data quality issue directly in Daniel's multispectral imagery and drone imagery coverage area.
artifacts.relevance_why: Direct: multispectral and drone imagery are in Daniel's stack; this is a practitioner signal about data quality issues that resonate with Globe & Atlas readers.

artifacts.title: How to Split GeoPandas LineStrings into 0.5-Mile Segments in Python
artifacts.desc: Community question with an answer about using GeoPandas and Shapely for road network segmentation — a practical Python/GeoPandas pattern directly applicable to Daniel's spatial data workflows and a potential short-form tutorial angle.
artifacts.relevance_why: Direct: GeoPandas is in Daniel's Python library stack; this segmentation pattern is reusable across road, pipeline, and utility network analysis workflows.

artifacts.title: NEW Open Claude Code — Fully Free Open-Source AI Coding Agent Tutorial
artifacts.desc: Reviews an open-source, free alternative to Claude Code CLI — important competitive signal for Daniel who relies on Claude Code daily and needs to assess whether free alternatives offer comparable capability for GIS scripting.
artifacts.relevance_why: Direct: Claude Code CLI is in Daniel's stack; evaluating open alternatives informs both his personal tooling decisions and Tool Critic content.

artifacts.title: xAI Will Be Dissolved as a Separate Entity
artifacts.desc: Widely shared report that xAI (Grok) is being folded back into X/Twitter as a unified entity — a market structure signal relevant to understanding the competitive LLM landscape Daniel monitors for Globe & Atlas.
artifacts.relevance_why: Adjacent: Daniel monitors competing LLM providers relative to his Claude API primary stack; xAI restructuring affects the competitive dynamics he covers in Industry Lens.

artifacts.title: ChatGPT Just Got More Dangerous — OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Cyber Tests
artifacts.desc: Reports on OpenAI quietly switching ChatGPT's default model and new cybersecurity capability benchmarks raising safety questions.

artifacts.title: If the EU Had Built Claude — Reddit Satire Thread
artifacts.desc: High-engagement satirical post imagining EU AI regulation applied to Claude — cultural signal about AI governance sentiment in the practitioner community.

artifacts.title: Local AI on Linux — What Is Local AI? LLMs in 5 Minutes
artifacts.desc: Introductory explainer on running local LLMs on Linux, aimed at IT practitioners new to the concept.

artifacts.title: AutoGPT — Accessible Autonomous Agent Platform
artifacts.desc: Long-running autonomous agent platform that pioneered the agentic-AI concept; now a mature Python framework with tooling for building and running self-directed agents.

artifacts.title: Dify — Production-Ready Agentic Workflow Development Platform
artifacts.desc: Open-source TypeScript/Python platform for building and deploying agentic workflows in production with visual tooling and multi-model support.

exercises.title: Test ArcGIS Pro AI Tool Finder on a Real FME-to-ArcGIS Workflow
exercises.desc: Open ArcGIS Pro and use the new AI assistant to find and run a spatial analysis tool you'd normally configure manually (e.g., zonal statistics on a Sentinel-2 raster). Document what it gets right and wrong — this is direct content for a Tool Critic post comparing AI-assisted vs manual ArcGIS Pro workflows.
exercises.source_artifact: Ask ArcGIS Pro AI to Find the Right Tool for You

exercises.title: Build a Minimal MCP Server That Exposes Your FME Workspace Catalog to Claude
exercises.desc: Follow the dev.to MCP server tutorial to scaffold a Python MCP server, then instead of file search, expose your local FME workspace directory so Claude Code can list, read, and query .fmw files. This makes Claude Code a genuine co-pilot for FME workflow authoring — a high-value Build pillar post.
exercises.source_artifact: Build Your Own MCP Server: A Repo-Agnostic File Search Tool for AI Assistants

exercises.title: Install everything-claude-code and claude-mem, Then Run a Multi-Session GIS Script Build
exercises.desc: Install both repos into your Claude Code CLI environment. Run a realistic multi-session task — e.g., building a Sentinel Hub API raster downloader across 3 separate Claude Code sessions — and measure how much context is recovered automatically by claude-mem vs a bare session. Write up results for a Build pillar post on 'Making Claude Code Remember Your GIS Projects.'
exercises.source_artifact: everything-claude-code — Agent Harness Performance Optimization System
